description:
The cut-over safety gate. By here the new machinery is fully built (.1-.3, kernel via .6) but nothing live reads it -- function_type_env and the old consumers are UNTOUCHED. Prove the new path fires correctly end-to-end, in the unique window where both paths coexist and can be diffed.

Drive a real fixture (quicksort, a spec_ok case). A test role-plays the soon-to-be-re-seated consumer by demanding the spec type names through the PRODUCTION demand path -- the same wait-on-TypeDefined call .4 will add to DeriveFunctionContract, no shim.

PERMANENT assertions (these become the oracle and stay):
  - DeriveTypeDef fires exactly once per referenced name; a @type no demand references stays cold.
  - the in-house resolver turns each spec into the expected hard Ty (the worked examples).

TEMPORARY cut-over scaffold (removed in .4 -- you cannot call a deleted function):
  - for every overlapping name, the new resolver Ty EQUALS the Ty function_type_env produces. This is the behavior-identical proof; it is only possible while both paths exist, which is exactly now. .4 deletes this half when it deletes function_type_env.

Green here means .4/.5 become a near-mechanical swap: correctness is already proven, so the cut-over is just flip + delete. Nothing is disrupted at any commit up to this point.
title: Dark-launch parity proof: new name->Ty path fires end-to-end with the old path untouched
